description:
You are the system administrator for a small web-hosting VPS.  
    The developers sometimes expose an internal **debug** web service on TCP port 8080 that must never be reachable from the public Internet.  
    To standardise how the firewall is configured you want every host to have a tiny helper script that, when executed, inserts a single iptables rule dropping any packets destined for TCP port 8080.

    Your assignment:

    1. Create the directory  
       /home/user/scripts  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Inside that directory create an executable file named  
       /home/user/scripts/block_web_debug_port.sh  

    3. The file **must contain exactly two lines**, nothing more and nothing less, in the order shown below (note the required single TAB before “iptables”, not spaces):

        Line 1: #!/bin/bash  
        Line 2: ⟨TAB⟩iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP  

       • “⟨TAB⟩” represents one literal tab character (ASCII 0x09).  
       • There must be a single trailing newline at the end of the second line.

    4. Make the script executable for its owner (mode 0755 is acceptable).

    5. Do **not** actually execute the script; just create it with the exact contents and permissions described.

    Verification format the grader will use:
    • It will check that the file exists at the precise path /home/user/scripts/block_web_debug_port.sh  
    • It will verify that “stat -c '%a' /home/user/scripts/block_web_debug_port.sh” prints a number whose last three digits are 755 or 775 (executable for owner).  
    • It will read the file and must match **byte-for-byte** the two-line template above, including the single tab character at the start of line 2 and a single newline character terminating the file.

    Hints:
    • You need only one shell command to fulfil all steps (e.g., using printf with the proper escape sequences and install or chmod), but choose whatever approach you prefer.  
    • Remember that paths are case-sensitive.
truth:
The grader will expect:

    1. Directory /home/user/scripts must exist (any permissions are fine as long as user can read it).
    2. File /home/user/scripts/block_web_debug_port.sh must exist.
    3. File permissions: -rwxr-xr-x (octal 0755) is accepted; group-write (0775) is also accepted as executable flag presence for owner is mandatory.
    4. Exact file contents (with one trailing newline):

        Line 1: "#!/bin/bash\n"
        Line 2: "\tiptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP\n"

       i.e. total characters count = 48 (including both newline characters and one tab character).
name: task_000000_d660ccf8
